content.description:
Navigation in Hobson determines which item is displayed and how. The system supports both full-page navigation and opening items as siblings within a container.

## URL Structure

The URL reflects the current view state:

```
https://hobson.app/#item-uuid
https://hobson.app/#item-uuid?view=view-uuid
https://hobson.app/?instance=nested#item-uuid
```

| Component | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|
| `#item-uuid` | The root item being displayed |
| `?view=` | Optional view override |
| `?instance=` | Nested instance prefix |

## The Viewport

The [viewport](item://88888888-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) is a special item managing view state:

- **rootId** — Currently displayed item
- **rootViewId** — Optional view override
- **selection** — Currently selected child (runtime only)

Viewport state persists to IndexedDB, so your location survives page refresh.

## Navigation Methods

### Full Navigation: `api.navigate()`

```javascript
// Navigate to an item
api.navigate('item-uuid');

// With view override
api.navigate('item-uuid', { view: 'view-uuid' });

// With scroll target (for code/markdown)
api.navigate('item-uuid', { 
  field: 'code',
  region: 'myFunction',
  line: 42
});
```

This changes the viewport root and updates the URL.

### Sibling Navigation: `api.siblingContainer`

Within a spatial container, items can open as siblings instead of replacing the view:

```javascript
// In a view's render function
if (api.siblingContainer) {
  // Open next to current item
  api.siblingContainer.addSibling('target-uuid');
} else {
  // Not in a container, do full navigation
  api.navigate('target-uuid');
}
```

This is how `item://` links work in markdown—they add siblings when possible.

## Link Syntax

Markdown supports `item://` links:

```markdown
[Display Text](item://item-uuid)
[Code Item](item://item-uuid#code?region=myFunction)
[Line 42](item://item-uuid#code?lines=42)
```

Click behavior:

| Action | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| Click | Open as sibling in container (or navigate if at root) |
| Alt+Click | Navigate as root in current tab |
| Ctrl/Cmd+Click | Open in new browser tab |
| Middle-click | Open in new browser tab |
| Right-click → Copy link | Copies a `?root=GUID` URL |

## Navigation Events

The system emits events on navigation:

- `viewport:root-changed` — Root item changed
- `selection:changed` — Selected child changed

Subscribe via the [event system](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) to react to navigation.

## Navigation Stack

Browser back/forward buttons work because:
1. Navigation updates `location.hash`
2. `hashchange` event triggers viewport update
3. Previous item renders

## Item Palette (Cmd+K)

The [item search modal](item://f1111111-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) provides quick navigation:

1. Press `Cmd+K` (or `Ctrl+K`)
2. Type to search items
3. Enter to navigate

## Design Rationale

### Why URL-Based Root?

The root item being displayed is specified via URL hash, enabling:
- **Bookmarking** — save a link to any item
- **Browser history** — back/forward buttons work naturally
- **Sharing** — copy URL to reference a specific item
- **Multiple entry points** — different bookmarks for different workflows

### Why Renderer-Controlled Navigation?

The kernel provides navigation helpers (`api.navigate()`, `api.siblingContainer`) but doesn't enforce a particular pattern. This means:
- Spatial containers open links as sibling windows
- Full-screen views do full navigation
- Future views could use browsing paths, split panes, or modals
- Navigation behavior is a view concern, not a kernel concern

## See Also

- [Viewport](item://c0c0c0c0-0033-0000-0000-000000000000) — The viewport subsystem
- [Context Menu](item://c0c0c0c0-0034-0000-0000-000000000000) — Right-click interaction
- [Spatial Windowing](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9) — How links work in spatial context
- [concept:attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) — Parent-child relationships
- [Transclusion](item://c0c0c0c0-0035-0000-0000-000000000000) — Embedding content from other items inline
